steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the parallel lines and transversal
steps.content: Line $AC$ cuts through the two parallel lines $BC$ and $AD$, making it a transversal. The angle $x^{\circ}$ at point $C$ (between $BC$ and $AC$) and the angle $y^{\circ}$ at point $A$ (between $AC$ and $AD$) are interior angles on opposite sides of the transversal.

steps.title: Apply the Alternate Interior Angles Theorem
steps.content:
When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, alternate interior angles are equal. Here, $\angle BCA = x^{\circ}$ and $\angle CAD = y^{\circ}$ are alternate interior angles. Therefore, $y = x$.

Since $x = 22$, we have $y = 22$. That confirms A is correct.
